After round 6, we have identified that the secret contains red, blue, orange, and green. Yellow and white are now confirmed impossible (along with black and purple). The exact positions are still not uniquely determined; two possible arrangements satisfy all feedback from rounds 1-6. The fifth position in the secret is still unknown and could be any color not in the impossible set (e.g., a duplicate of red/blue/orange/green or a new untested color). Confidence is high that these four colors are correct, but final assignment requires one more round to distinguish between the two candidate configurations.

Position0=orange is deduced from multiple rounds and constraints. The fifth position X is free subject to not being blue or any impossible color. All prior rounds are satisfied uniquely by this arrangement.

Round 8 confirms the secret structure as [orange, blue, green, red, X] where X is an unknown color. All prior rounds are satisfied with 100% consistency. The only remaining unknown is the color at position 4. With no further constraints, X could be any color except impossible ones (yellow, white, black, purple) and blue.
